name: Chris Yamamoto

career_goals.primary: New-grad Software Engineer at a top-tier tech company, working on infrastructure / distributed systems
career_goals.industry_preference: large-scale distributed systems, infrastructure, systems software
career_goals.timeline: Apply Fall 2026 for new-grad SWE, start summer 2027 after graduation

background.work_status: summer 2025 SWE intern at a mid-sized fintech (received return offer, declining); part-time research assistant for the OS lab during the school year
background.family_background: From the Boston area. Both parents are software engineers. Has been programming since middle school.

perspective: Knows exactly what they want and why. Comfortable with low-level systems work; reads kernel mailing lists for fun. Concerns about whether course assignments produce portfolio artifacts that read as 'real' to FAANG-tier interviewers — worried xv6-only labs look toy compared to 'real Linux' work peers do at internships. Wants augmentations that produce concrete artifacts (technical writeups, public repo, performance numbers) they can point to on a resume or in an interview. Will push back if augmentation feels like busywork rather than competency-building.
